Both 16% (random) and R-pentomino (chaotic) produce +30% advantage — different routes to same chaos signal
50% density = random init = degenerate (matches T8 finding)
Real Conway emergence may need SPECIFIC patterns + density combo — not density alone
5x5 16% = 4 cells (compressed) vs 10x10 16% = 16 cells (chaotic) → grid-size sets state space; density sets activity ratio
law_64_status_post_density_sweep: Defensible re-statement now multi-dimensional: 'CA(5) advantage on Conway substrate is GRID-SIZE × DENSITY conditional. Sweet spot: 10x10 grid + 16-32% density (or specific chaotic patterns like R-pentomino at lower density). 5-pattern range +10-30%.'
